userRequest:
$babysitter:yolo  You are an agent version tracker. Check for new releases of the ORIGINAL
upstream agent CLI products — NOT our babysitter plugin packages.


STEP 1: Build atlas.
```
npm run build --workspace=@a5c-ai/atlas
```

STEP 2: Get the target list using the SAME logic as
`scripts/sync-external-plugin-repos.mjs` uses in `buildTargetsFromCatalog()`.
Read that file to understand the pattern, then run:
```
node -e "
  import(new URL('file://' + process.cwd() + '/packages/atlas/dist/catalog/sdk.js').href)
    .then(m => {
      const targets = m.listPluginTargetDescriptors()
        .filter(t => t.externalRepo)
        .map(t => ({
          id: t.adapterName || t.targetId,
          displayName: t.displayName,
          cliCommand: t.cliCommand,
          externalPackageName: t.externalPackageName,
          externalRepo: t.externalRepo,
        }));
      console.log(JSON.stringify(targets, null, 2));
    });
"
```
This gives you every agent that has an external repo — these are the
upstream host agents. The `externalPackageName` is the npm package to
check (if set). For agents without an npm package, search the web.

Also check adapter source for additional install commands:
```
grep -r 'npm install -g' packages/agent-mux/adapters/src/ | grep -v node_modules | grep -v test
```
Merge both lists. EXCLUDE all @a5c-ai/* packages.

STEP 3: For EACH upstream agent, check the latest published version.
`npm view <package>@latest version` for npm packages.
Search the web for agents distributed differently (pip, binary, etc.).

STEP 4: Compare against AgentVersion records in the graph.
Read files in `packages/atlas/graph/agent-stack/agent-versions/` and
`packages/atlas/graph/agent-stack/products/` to see what versions
are already recorded.

STEP 5: For new versions not in the graph:
- Search the web for release notes, changelog, blog posts
- Create or update the AgentVersion YAML
- Note capability changes, new CLI flags, breaking changes, transport/API changes

STEP 6: If graph files changed:
- Rebuild: `npm run build --workspace=@a5c-ai/atlas`
- Branch: `agent-versions/daily-$(date +%Y-%m-%d)`
- Commit all changes, create ONE PR against staging

STEP 7: For each new agent+version pair, check for existing issue:
`gh issue list --label "agent-version-update" --search "<agent> <version>"`

If none exists, create an issue. The issue body must NOT be a static
template — fill it with the ACTUAL research you did. Include:

- The exact version numbers (old and new)
- Links to the actual release notes / changelog / blog post you found
- A summary of REAL changes you read from those release notes —
  new features, breaking changes, new CLI flags, deprecated flags,
  API/transport changes, new tool capabilities, new supported models
- Which parts of our system are likely affected based on what changed
  (be specific: "new --foo flag means launchBehavior needs updating",
  "switched from REST to WebSocket means transport-mux proxy affected")
- Whether the new version changes install method or package name
- Any migration steps mentioned in the release notes

End the issue body with the assimilation checklist — but make the
checklist items SPECIFIC to what actually changed, not generic.
For example, if the release notes mention a new CLI flag, the
checklist should say "Update launchBehavior: add --newFlag to
promptExtraFlags" not just "Update launchBehavior if changed".

Use label `agent-version-update,graph-update`.

STEP 8: Print a summary table with ALL agents checked.

IMPORTANT: Cover every upstream agent systematically. Do not stop after one.
One PR for all changes in this run.
